test.command: swift test --package-path OpenBurnBarCore && swift test --package-path OpenBurnBarDaemon && xcodebuild test -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-app-tests-scrutiny CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO -only-testing:"OpenBurnBarTests"

typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck-scrutiny CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit evidence of all mandated context reads (mission AGENTS.md plus required .factory/library files) before allowing followedProcedure=true in handoffs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three fix reviews in this round reported missing required reads in transcript skeletons (core-engine-fix-active-profile-legacy-multirow-hydration, core-engine-fix-browser-launch-endlaunch-failure-paths, core-engine-harden-projection-minimal-write-regression-signal).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce that verification.commandsRun includes concrete content-read evidence (Read/Grep/file-content commands), not only test commands, whenever skills require pre-read context files.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for core-engine-fix-active-profile-legacy-multirow-hydration and core-engine-fix-browser-launch-endlaunch-failure-paths observed followedProcedure=true handoffs whose verification.commandsRun listed tests only and no context-read evidence.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate reports of missing .factory/library reads across multiple feature reviews.
Timestamp-tie nondeterminism in legacy active-row canonical selection.
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking code concern captured in feature review; not a shared-state guidance update
